# Marquee

A scrolling content banner compound component that continuously animates its children across the viewport. Useful for announcements, news tickers, logo banners, or decorative scrolling content.

## Import

```jsx
import { Marquee } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Usage Pattern

This component uses a nested property pattern. Access sub-components as properties of the main component:

```jsx
<Marquee>
  <Marquee.Edge side="start" />
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item />
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
  <Marquee.Edge side="end" />
</Marquee>
```

## Sub-components

The Marquee is composed of five sub-components that must be used together:

| Component | Chakra Equivalent | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Marquee` | `Marquee.Root` | Root container that controls the animation behavior and configuration. |
| `Marquee.Viewport` | `Marquee.Viewport` | The visible scrolling area that clips overflowing content. |
| `Marquee.Content` | `Marquee.Content` | Wraps the scrollable content and handles the animation loop. |
| `Marquee.Item` | `Marquee.Item` | An individual item within the scrolling content. |
| `Marquee.Edge` | `Marquee.Edge` | Renders a gradient fade effect on a specified side of the marquee. |

### Anatomy

```jsx
<Marquee>
  <Marquee.Edge side="start" />      {/* optional */}
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item>...</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item>...</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
  <Marquee.Edge side="end" />         {/* optional */}
</Marquee>
```

## Props

### Marquee (Root)

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `speed` | `number` | `50` | The speed of the animation in pixels per second. |
| `side` | `"start" \| "end" \| "top" \| "bottom"` | `"start"` | The side/direction the marquee scrolls towards. Use `"top"` or `"bottom"` for vertical scrolling. |
| `reverse` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether to reverse the animation direction. |
| `autoFill` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether to automatically duplicate content to fill the container. |
| `spacing` | `string` | `"1rem"` | The spacing between marquee items. |
| `pauseOnInteraction` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether to pause the animation on user interaction (hover, focus). |
| `paused` | `boolean` | - | Controlled pause state. |
| `defaultPaused` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the marquee is paused by default. |
| `delay` | `number` | `0` | The delay before the animation starts (in seconds). |
| `loopCount` | `number` | `0` | The number of times to loop the animation. `0` means infinite. |
| `onLoopComplete` | `() => void` | - | Callback fired when the marquee completes one loop iteration. |
| `onComplete` | `() => void` | - | Callback fired when the marquee completes all loops. Only fires for finite loops (`loopCount > 0`). |
| `onPauseChange` | `(details) => void` | - | Callback fired when the pause status changes. |
| `unstyled` | `boolean` | - | Whether to remove the component's default styles. |

### Marquee.Edge

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `side` | `"start" \| "end"` | **required** | The side where the gradient fade effect should appear. |

### Marquee.Content, Marquee.Viewport, Marquee.Item

These sub-components accept standard HTML div props. They serve as structural wrappers and do not have component-specific props beyond `asChild` and `as`.

## Usage

### Basic Marquee

```jsx
<Marquee>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item>Item 1</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item>Item 2</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item>Item 3</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Marquee with Pause on Interaction

```jsx
<Marquee pauseOnInteraction speed={50}>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 1</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 2</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 3</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 4</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Reverse Direction

```jsx
<Marquee reverse autoFill>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 1</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 2</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 3</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Auto-Fill Content

Automatically duplicates content to fill the container width.

```jsx
<Marquee autoFill spacing="2rem">
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">
        <Image src="/logos/partner1.svg" alt="Partner 1" h="8" />
      </Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">
        <Image src="/logos/partner2.svg" alt="Partner 2" h="8" />
      </Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">
        <Image src="/logos/partner3.svg" alt="Partner 3" h="8" />
      </Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Edge Gradients

Use `Marquee.Edge` to add fade-out gradient effects on the sides.

```jsx
<Marquee>
  <Marquee.Edge side="start" />
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 1</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 2</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 3</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
  <Marquee.Edge side="end" />
</Marquee>
```

### News Ticker

```jsx
const newsItems = [
  "Breaking news: Important announcement",
  "Market update: Stocks rally",
  "Tech: New product launch",
];

<Marquee>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content textStyle="sm">
      {newsItems.map((item, i) => (
        <Marquee.Item key={i} pr="4">
          <HStack gap="8" fontWeight="medium" textTransform="uppercase">
            {item}
            <Circle size="1" bg="colorPalette.solid" />
          </HStack>
        </Marquee.Item>
      ))}
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Vertical Scrolling

Use `side="top"` or `side="bottom"` to scroll vertically.

```jsx
<Marquee side="bottom" height="300px" spacing="2rem">
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item py="2">Vertical Item 1</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item py="2">Vertical Item 2</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item py="2">Vertical Item 3</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Finite Loop with Callbacks

```jsx
<Marquee
  loopCount={3}
  onLoopComplete={() => console.log("Loop completed")}
  onComplete={() => console.log("All loops finished")}
>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 1</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 2</Marquee.Item>
      <Marquee.Item px="2rem">Item 3</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```

### Custom Speed

```jsx
{/* Slow */}
<Marquee speed={25}>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item>Slow scrolling</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>

{/* Fast */}
<Marquee speed={100}>
  <Marquee.Viewport>
    <Marquee.Content>
      <Marquee.Item>Fast scrolling</Marquee.Item>
    </Marquee.Content>
  </Marquee.Viewport>
</Marquee>
```
